title: telc Deutsch A1
description: The telc Deutsch A1 exam is for anyone who has attended about 100-150 hours of German lessons. It is a general language exam.

structure.name: Hören (Listening)
structure.description: Listen to short everyday texts and announcements.

parts.title: Teil 1: Dialogues (Dialoge)
parts.description: Listen to 6 short dialogues and answer multiple-choice questions (visual/text). Each heard twice.

parts.title: Teil 2: Announcements (Ansagen)
parts.description: Listen to 4 announcements (e.g., at a station) and decide if True/False. Each heard once.

parts.title: Teil 3: Messages (Nachrichten)
parts.description: Listen to 5 short telephone announcements/messages and answer MCQs. Each heard twice.

structure.name: Lesen & Sprachbausteine
structure.description: Read short emails, ads, and grammar tasks.

parts.title: Teil 1: Correspondence (E-Mails)
parts.description: Read 2 short emails/notes and answer 5 questions (MCQ/True-False).

parts.title: Teil 2: Information (Anzeigen)
parts.description: Scan advertisements (e.g., in a newspaper) and match correct info to 5 situations.

parts.title: Teil 3: Announcements (Aushänge)
parts.description: Read notices, signs, or posters and answer 5 True/False questions.

parts.title: Sprachbausteine: Cloze Test
parts.description: Complete a short text with the correct grammar/vocabulary choices (6 items).

structure.name: Schreiben (Writing)
structure.description: Fill out a form and write a short personal note.

parts.title: Part 1: Form Filling (Formular)
parts.description: Read a situation and fill in a form (e.g., for a hotel) with 5 missing items.

parts.title: Task 2: Personal Note (Mitteilung)
parts.description: Write a short personal email or note (approx. 30 words) responding to 3 points in a prompt.

structure.name: Sprechen (Speaking)
structure.description: Interact in a group of participants.

parts.title: Teil 1: Introduction (Vorstellen)
parts.description: Introduce yourself (Name, Age, Country, etc.) and answer follow-up questions from the examiner.

parts.title: Teil 2: Information Exchange (Austauschen)
parts.description: Pick cards with themes and ask/answer questions with your partner.

parts.title: Teil 3: Requests & Responses (Bitten)
parts.description: Pick cards with images and formulate requests/commands; respond to partner.

tips: Arrive at least 15 minutes before the exam starts.
Know the exam format perfectly (number of parts, timing).
Practice with official model tests to build confidence.
Master time management: never leave a question blank.
Reading/Listening: Read questions/options before text/audio starts.
Writing: Use standard connectors (weil, obwohl, deshalb) as shortcuts.
Speaking: Clear communication is better than 100% perfect grammar.
Speaking: Use safe phrases (Redemittel) to stay fluent.

resources.title: Information and practice tests
resources.description: Information and practice tests for telc Deutsch A1 exam

title: telc Deutsch A2
description: Tests your ability to express yourself in a simple way in everyday and professional situations.

structure.name: Hören (Listening)
structure.description: Listen to phone messages, news bits, and dialogues.

parts.title: Teil 1: Phone Notes (Notizen)
parts.description: Listen to 5 short telephone announcements and complete missing information in phone notes (e.g., name, time, number). Heard twice.

parts.title: Teil 2: News Snippets (Nachrichten)
parts.description: Listen to  radio announcements or news snippets and answer 5 multiple-choice questions (a, b, c). Heard once.

parts.title: Teil 3: Dialogues (Gespräche)
parts.description: Listen to a longer conversation (e.g., planning a trip) and match 5 pictures or statements to the correct person. Heard twice.

structure.name: Lesen & Sprachbausteine
structure.description: Read floor plans, ads, and solve grammar tasks.

parts.title: Teil 1: Matching (Zuordnung)
parts.description: Read a department store floor plan or complex directory and match 5 requests to the correct locations (MCQ a, b, c).

parts.title: Teil 2: Selective Reading (Anzeigen)
parts.description: Read 5 newspaper or magazine ads/snippets and answer 5 multiple-choice questions (a, b, c).

parts.title: Teil 3: Detailed Reading (Mitteilungen)
parts.description: Read 5 short notices, signs, or emails and decide for each if a given statement is True or False.

parts.title: Sprachbausteine: Grammar & Vocabulary
parts.description: Complete two short texts with the correct grammar/vocabulary choices (10 items total).

structure.name: Schreiben (Writing)
structure.description: Fill out a form and write a semi-formal message.

parts.title: Teil 1: Form Filling (Formular)
parts.description: Read information about a person and use it to complete a form (e.g., service request) with 5 details.

parts.title: Teil 2: Personal Message (Brief)
parts.description: Write a personal email or letter (approx. 40 words) covering 3 specific bullet points provided.

structure.name: Pause
structure.description: Break between written and oral part.

structure.name: Sprechen (Speaking)
structure.description: Participate in a pair exam with no preparation time.

parts.title: Teil 1: Self-Introduction (Vorstellen)
parts.description: Introduce yourself using keywords (Name, Born, Family, Hobbies) and answer examiner questions.

parts.title: Teil 2: Everyday Topic (Alltagsgespräch)
parts.description: Alltagsgespräch: Engage in a dialogue with your partner about an everyday topic using prompt cards.

parts.title: Teil 3: Planning Together (Planen)
parts.description: Negotiate or plan an activity together with your partner and reach an agreement.

tips: Arrive at least 15 minutes before the exam starts.
Know the exam format perfectly (number of parts, timing).
Practice with official model tests to build confidence.
Master time management: never leave a question blank.
Reading/Listening: Read questions/options before text/audio starts.
Writing: Use standard connectors (weil, obwohl, deshalb) as shortcuts.
Speaking: Clear communication is better than 100% perfect grammar.
Speaking: Use safe phrases (Redemittel) to stay fluent.

resources.title: Information and practice tests
resources.description: Information and practice tests for telc Deutsch A2 exam

title: telc Deutsch B1
description: Sound basic knowledge of German for independent communication.

structure.name: Lesen & Sprachbausteine
structure.description: Read blog posts, ads, and grammar tasks.

parts.title: Teil 1: Global Reading (Globalverstehen)
parts.description: Read 5 short texts and match them to 5 corresponding headlines.

parts.title: Teil 2: Detailed Reading (Detailverstehen)
parts.description: Read an article or report and answer 5 multiple-choice questions (a, b, c).

parts.title: Teil 3: Selective Reading (Anzeigen)
parts.description: Identify 10 suitable advertisements for 10 specific people/situations.

parts.title: Teil 1: Grammar (Grammatik)
parts.description: Complete a letter/email with 10 correct words (multiple choice from 3 options each).

parts.title: Teil 2: Vocabulary (Wortschatz)
parts.description: Complete a text with 10 correct words from a provided list of 15 words.

structure.name: Hören (Listening)
structure.description: Understand global and detailed info from audio.

parts.title: Teil 1: Global Listening (Globalverstehen)
parts.description: Listen to 5 announcements (e.g., at an airport) and decide if True/False. Heard once.

parts.title: Teil 2: Selective Listening (Gespräch)
parts.description: Listen to an interview or report and answer 10 True/False questions. Heard once.

parts.title: Teil 3: Detailed Listening (Dialoge)
parts.description: Listen to 10 short dialogues and decide if statements are True/False. Heard once.

parts.title: Part 1: Email/Letter (E-Mail/Brief)
parts.description: Write an email or letter (approx. 80-100 words) responding to 4 bullet points in a situation.

parts.title: Teil 1: Getting to Know (Kontaktaufnahme)
parts.description: Kontaktaufnahme: Get to know your partner by asking questions about their life/hobbies.

parts.title: Teil 2: Topic Presentation (Thema)
parts.description: Talk about a topic (e.g., an experience or opinion) using provided prompts.

parts.title: Teil 3: Planning Together (Planen)
parts.description: Plan an activity or solve a task together with your partner.

tips: Arrive at least 15 minutes before the exam starts.
Know the exam format perfectly (number of parts, timing).
Practice with official model tests to build confidence.
Master time management: never leave a question blank.
Reading/Listening: Read questions/options before text/audio starts.
Writing: Use standard connectors (weil, obwohl, deshalb) as shortcuts.
Speaking: Clear communication is better than 100% perfect grammar.
Speaking: Use safe phrases (Redemittel) to stay fluent.

resources.title: Information and practice tests
resources.description: Information and practice tests for telc Deutsch B1 exam

title: telc Deutsch B2
description: Advanced proficiency for professional and academic contexts.

structure.name: Lesen & Sprachbausteine
structure.description: Complex reading tasks and advanced grammar.

parts.title: Teil 1: Global Reading (Globalverstehen)
parts.description: Global comprehension: Match 5 headlines to 5 short newspaper articles.

parts.title: Teil 2: Detailed Reading (Detailverstehen)
parts.description: Detailed comprehension: Read a longer text and answer 5 multiple-choice questions (a, b, c).

parts.title: Teil 3: Selective Reading (Anzeigen)
parts.description: Selective comprehension: Identify 10 advertisements that match 10 specific situations.

parts.title: Teil 1: Grammar (Grammatik)
parts.description: Complete a formal letter/text with 10 correct words (MCQ from 3 options).

parts.title: Teil 2: Vocabulary (Wortschatz)
parts.description: Complete a specialized text with 10 correct words from a list of 15.

parts.title: Teil 1: Opinion Matching (Meinungen)
parts.description: Listen to 5 people stating their opinions on a topic and match statements. Heard once.

parts.title: Teil 2: Global/Detailed (Interview)
parts.description: Listen to a radio interview or discussion and answer 10 True/False questions. Heard once.

parts.title: Teil 3: Selective Listening (Nachrichten)
parts.description: Listen to 5 short reports/announcements and answer 5 questions (MCQ/TF). Heard once.

structure.name: Schreiben (Writing)
structure.description: Write a formal message or response.

parts.title: Task 1: Formal Message (Brief/Mail)
parts.description: Write a formal letter/email (at least 150 words) addressing 4 mandatory points.

structure.name: Sprechen (Speaking)
structure.description: Discuss topics and negotiate in pairs.

parts.title: Teil 1: Topic Presentation (Vorstellen)
parts.description: Presentation: Present a topic or experience clearly for about 2 minutes.

parts.title: Teil 2: Discussion (Diskussion)
parts.description: Discussion: Discuss a controversial topic with your partner, expressing your opinion.

parts.title: Teil 3: Problem Solving (Verhandeln)
parts.description: Problem Solving: Reach an agreement with your partner on a planning task.

tips: Arrive at least 15 minutes before the exam starts.
Know the exam format perfectly (number of parts, timing).
Practice with official model tests to build confidence.
Master time management: never leave a question blank.
Reading/Listening: Read questions/options before text/audio starts.
Writing: Use standard connectors (weil, obwohl, deshalb) as shortcuts.
Speaking: Clear communication is better than 100% perfect grammar.
Speaking: Use safe phrases (Redemittel) to stay fluent.

resources.title: Information and practice tests
resources.description: Information and practice tests for telc Deutsch B2 exam
